mnemonics: SCALED DATA: RDBMS is Structured, Centralized, ACID, Limited, Expensive, and Designed for small data. Hadoop is for Huge, Accessible, Distributed, Open-source, Unstructured/structured, and Petabyte-scale data.
keyPoints: RDBMS is designed for structured data and transactional integrity (ACID compliance), while Hadoop is designed for massive volumes of unstructured and structured data (scale-out architecture).
RDBMS uses a schema-on-write approach, requiring a predefined schema before loading data. Hadoop uses a schema-on-read approach, offering flexibility for data of unknown structure.
RDBMS is typically scaled vertically (adding more power to a single server), whereas Hadoop is scaled horizontally (adding more commodity servers to a cluster).
commonMistakes: Assuming Hadoop is a direct replacement for an RDBMS; they are complementary technologies for different data problems.
Believing that Hadoop does not support transactions or data integrity; tools like HBase and frameworks built on YARN can provide these capabilities for specific needs.
